Māhitahi & Our Values

Learn about the Māori values of collaboration and knowledge-sharing that power KeteConnect.

Read Our Story →
Our Foundation

Tikanga Māori — Our Guiding Values

KeteConnect is built on the wisdom of te ao Māori. These values guide how we create community.

🌿
Whanaungatanga
Relationships & Kinship
Building genuine connections between students, creating a sense of belonging and mutual care — because you are not alone in this journey.
🧺
Kete Mātauranga
The Basket of Knowledge
Sharing knowledge freely and openly. Like the kete of Māori tradition, knowledge grows when given and received generously.
💬
Kōrero
Communication & Dialogue
Meaningful conversation and listening with intent. We foster spaces for authentic kōrero between students from all walks of life.
🤝
Manaakitanga
Hospitality & Respect
Uplifting the mana (dignity) of others through kindness and inclusion. Everyone deserves to feel welcome here.

Ready to find your Whānau?

Join thousands of students already connecting, learning, and thriving on KeteConnect.

© 2026 KeteConnect. Built with aroha 💚

Māhitahi — Working Together

Discover the Māori concept of collaboration at the heart of KeteConnect, and how our platform connects to a greater vision for student wellbeing and success.

The Concept

What is Māhitahi?

Māhitahi (pronounced maa-hee-tah-hee) is a te reo Māori word meaning "working together as one" — a philosophy of unified collaboration rooted in the Māori worldview. It goes beyond simple teamwork; it embodies the idea that collective effort, shared purpose, and mutual support produce outcomes far greater than any individual could achieve alone.

In the context of a university, Māhitahi calls us to break down the barriers of isolation that many first-year students experience — the silence of a lecture hall, the loneliness of a new city, the struggle of an unfamiliar subject — and replace them with connection, cooperation, and communal strength.

KeteConnect was designed around this very principle: that when students come together — across cultures, disciplines, and backgrounds — they do not just survive university; they flourish.

"He aha te mea nui o te ao? He tangata, he tangata, he tangata."

— What is the greatest thing in the world? It is people, it is people, it is people.
🌿

Māhitahi in Practice

Every study group formed, every event attended, every meal shared at a campus food stall — these are all expressions of Māhitahi. KeteConnect makes these moments visible, accessible, and meaningful.

Core Principles

Key Aspects of Māhitahi

The five dimensions of working together that KeteConnect is built upon.

01

Kotahitanga — Unity

Bringing diverse people into a single, unified purpose. KeteConnect creates a shared digital marae where every student — regardless of background — belongs and contributes.

02

Tūāohangatanga — Mutual Support

Actively supporting one another's wellbeing and academic journey. Study groups, activity partnerships, and peer-to-peer mentorship are all expressions of this value.

03

Mahi Tahi — Shared Work

Distributing effort and sharing the load. Whether tackling a difficult assignment or organising a campus event, no one has to do it alone on KeteConnect.

04

Rangatiratanga — Leadership

Empowering students to lead. Study group facilitators, event organisers, and food stall hosts all exercise rangatiratanga — self-determination through purposeful leadership.

05

Aroha — Compassion

The heart of all collaboration. Aroha (love and compassion) ensures that KeteConnect remains a safe, inclusive, and genuinely caring community — not just a transactional platform.

The Meaning

What is Kete Mātauranga?

In Māori tradition, a kete is a woven basket — a vessel for holding precious things. Mātauranga means knowledge, wisdom, and understanding. Together, Kete Mātauranga refers to "the basket of knowledge" — a concept describing how wisdom is gathered, held, and shared within a community.

According to Māori cosmology, Tāne-nui-ā-Rangi ascended to the heavens and returned with three kete of knowledge: te kete tuauri (ritual knowledge), te kete tuatea (harmful knowledge), and te kete aronui (knowledge that benefits all people). KeteConnect draws from this last kete — knowledge that uplifts, connects, and empowers.

Our platform is itself a kete — a digital woven basket that students fill with resources, connections, event experiences, and shared wisdom. The more students contribute, the richer the kete becomes for everyone.

🧺

Kete Mātauranga

The Basket of Knowledge — a vessel for wisdom, shared freely among all who seek to learn and grow together.

Knowledge Type
Te Kete Aronui — Knowledge that benefits all people
Our Commitment
Open, shared, community-centred learning
The Platform

What is KeteConnect?

More than an app — a movement to reimagine how students connect at university.

🎯

The Problem We Solve

First-year students face an invisible wall: social isolation, academic overwhelm, and a lack of belonging. They don't know where to eat, who to study with, or what events are happening. KeteConnect tears down that wall.

💡

The Opportunity

There is a significant gap in the market for a culturally-grounded, student-first community platform at New Zealand universities. KeteConnect fills this gap by combining technology with tikanga Māori — proving digital tools can honour indigenous values.

🌟

Our Connection to Kete Mātauranga

Just as a kete becomes more valuable the more it holds, KeteConnect grows richer with every student who joins, every study group formed, and every piece of knowledge shared. We are building the university's collective kete — together.

🎯

Our Objective Statement

To empower first-year university students in Aotearoa New Zealand by creating a digitally inclusive, culturally grounded community platform — rooted in the values of Māhitahi, Kete Mātauranga, and Whanaungatanga — that reduces social isolation, supports academic achievement, and fosters a genuine sense of belonging across diverse student communities.
